01 / Case Study

NetElixir

Role In-house Designer
Timeline 2023 – 2024
Tools Figma, Adobe CC, Webflow
Deliverables Landing Pages, Brand Assets, LinkedIn Content
Hero Visual / 01
Role
In-house Designer
Timeline
2023 – 2024
Tools
Figma, Adobe CC, Webflow
Deliverables
Landing Pages, Brand Assets, LinkedIn Content
01 / Overview

The Project

Translating an AI-powered marketing platform into a visual identity that earns immediate trust from enterprise clients.

NetElixir is a digital marketing agency with an in-house AI platform called LXRInsights. Working directly with the founder and product manager, the goal was to elevate the brand's visual presence across paid campaigns, the web, and organic social — all while staying deeply aligned with the product's positioning as a data-driven, premium tool.

02 / Challenge

The Problem

“The design had to communicate cutting-edge AI capability without losing the warmth and approachability the sales team depended on.”

Enterprise marketing tools often default to cold, overly technical aesthetics that alienate the mid-market buyers NetElixir was targeting. The challenge was finding a visual language that felt intelligent and rigorous without becoming sterile — all within a tight content calendar and without an established brand system to build from.

Research Frame / 01
03 / Process

How We Got There

01

Discovery & Research

Placeholder — describe what research or discovery activities were conducted. Who was interviewed? What data was collected? What did you learn that surprised you and shifted the direction of the project?

Discovery Frame / 01
02

Strategy & Direction

Placeholder — explain the strategic framework or creative direction that emerged from research. How did you translate findings into a clear brief or design direction? What decisions were made and why?

Strategy Frame / 02
03

Design & Execution

Placeholder — walk through the design phase. What were the key decisions in layout, hierarchy, color, and type? How did early explorations evolve? What feedback shaped the final direction?

Design Frame / 03
04

Iteration & Refinement

Placeholder — describe the revision cycle. How many rounds of feedback were there? What changed between versions? What constraints forced creative problem-solving?

Iteration Frame / 04

05 / Outcomes

The Impact

—
Metric One

Placeholder — describe a specific, measurable outcome tied to this project. Quantify where possible.

—
Metric Two

Placeholder — another key result. Focus on business or user impact, not just design outputs.

—
Metric Three

Placeholder — a third outcome. Could be qualitative — stakeholder feedback, brand perception shift, team adoption.

06 / Reflection

What I Learned

Placeholder — one or two sentences capturing the most important lesson or insight from this project.

Placeholder — expand on what you'd do differently, what skills grew, or what surprised you. Be honest and specific. Recruiters and clients respond to genuine reflection far more than polished PR. What constraint became an advantage? What assumption turned out to be wrong?
